content:
Option B: Create Something Entirely New

A short theatrical work (25–40 minutes) conceived from scratch as participatory narrative. You write the core material; we iterate on parameter mapping in real time. The system becomes a collaborator in your creative process, not a constraint imposed after the fact. You’d be inventing new dramaturgical territory—work that couldn’t exist without real-time audience input.

Option C: Experiment with Form

A series of micro-pieces (5–10 minutes each), each testing a different hypothesis about audience agency and narrative. Rapid prototyping. Failures are data. This mode is highest risk but also highest learning density. You’d be establishing principles through experimentation rather than argumentation.

Format: Iterative. You write; we prototype with audiences (10–50 people per test); you observe and adjust in real time. Video recordings of each test session. Weekly synthesis conversations. Google Docs becomes a living lab notebook where I document what I’m seeing and ask “Why did that work?” and “Why did that fail?”
